findings.description: The document title block in the PDF is centered, while in the HTML it is left-aligned.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'FACT SHEET / DRAFT PERMIT RENEWAL / Safety-Kleen Archdale / ...' centered on the page. HTML shows these lines left-aligned.

findings.description: Section headings 'Background' and 'Public Comment & Public Hearing' are underlined in the PDF but rendered as larger bold headings without underline in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF: underlined headings in body text size. HTML: larger bold headings (h2-style) without underline.

findings.description: The header block on page 2 is centered in the PDF but left-aligned in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF: centered 'FACT SHEET / DRAFT PERMIT RENEWAL / Safety-Kleen Archdale / ...' at top. HTML: left-aligned header block.

findings.description: The mailing address block for Mr. Adam Ulishney is indented in the PDF but left-aligned in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF shows the address block indented from the left margin. HTML shows it flush left.

findings.description: The URL in the PDF is presented as a hyperlink but is rendered as plain text in the HTML without an anchor tag.
findings.evidence: PDF contains 'https://edocs.deq.nc.gov/WasteManagement/Welcome.aspx' as a hyperlink. HTML renders it as plain text without an <a> tag.

findings.description: There is no h1 element in the HTML. The document title/header information is placed in a div with class 'page-header' using paragraph tags. The section headings 'Background' and 'Public Comment & Public Hearing' are rendered as h2 but there is no h1 to establish the hierarchy.
findings.evidence: The HTML uses <div class='page-header'> for the document title block and <h2> for 'Background' and 'Public Comment & Public Hearing' but has no <h1> element.

findings.description: The page header block (FACT SHEET, DRAFT PERMIT RENEWAL, Safety-Kleen Archdale, address, EPA ID) is repeated as both the outer page-header div and the mid-document page-header div, reflecting the two-page PDF headers. This is acceptable consolidation but the second occurrence breaks the flow of the sentence that spans across pages.
findings.evidence: The page header appears at the top and again mid-document before page 2 content, mirroring the PDF's two-page layout.

findings.description: There is a nested <main> element inside the outer <main> element, which is invalid HTML structure.
findings.evidence: The outer <body> contains <main>, then the content has another <main> nested inside it.
